goal: Pin: add commit-subject match strategy + guard against stale-binary regression in hook installer

summary.title: Pin: subject match strategy + session-start stale-binary guard
summary.what: Two related fixes to keep the team's intent state honest after a merge: a new `subject` strategy in the Pin cascade (between commit_hash and goal_text) so that intents whose feature branches were rebased before merging still get auto-pinned to the correct main commit; and an engine.BinaryStaleness() check whose result the hooks dispatcher prepends to the cursor session_start additional_context when the running binary's mtime predates synced main HEAD by more than 5 minutes. Cleanup: abandoned int_89dab5f5 (auto-Start regression's victim) plus int_625ea53e and int_7b161748 (duplicate early seals superseded by int_8b41cf41).
summary.why: After PR #38 (`hooks redesign as context provider`) merged, two of its own intents (int_3814973e agent-hooks-subsystem, int_4e6bbd80 smoke-test fixes) stayed stuck in `proposed` because the feature branch was rebased onto origin/main right before merge — every commit got a fresh tree+hash, so tree_hash and commit_hash strategies missed; goal_text also missed because the LLM-written goals are not substrings of the conventional-commit subjects. Concurrently, the auto-Start-on-prompt logic that PR #38 explicitly killed silently regressed in a fresh session because the hook wrapper was still on the pre-merge binary — the user's *question text* was minted as int_89dab5f5's goal. Both failures share a root cause: state doesn't catch up with reality when reality is a rebased commit or a not-yet-rebuilt binary. Fix the data path (subject strategy) and the visibility path (stale-binary warning) together so the same dogfood session that reproduces the bug also exercises the fix.
summary.user_goal: 为啥我的分支已经合并了 main 分支，但是有几个 intent 却仍然还是 proposed 的？看看怎么回事，切个新分支修一下，另外我们已经支持了 hook 方式使用 mainline，你在本次修复任务中考虑到 dogfood 的思路，看看 hook+mainline 这块有没有啥问题或者可以改进优化的点

decisions.point: How to pin intents whose feature branch was rebased before merge
decisions.chose: Add `subject` matching to the Pin cascade between commit_hash and goal_text
decisions.rationale: Subject is the property that survives a clean rebase intact (`git rebase` preserves messages by default). Exact match on the first line of code_commit's message vs an exact match against any LogEntry.Subject in the lookback window — more specific than goal_text substring search, less specific than tree/hash. Verified end-to-end: int_3814973e -> 93aeca9 and int_4e6bbd80 -> 86aceb9 via match_strategy=subject.

decisions.chose: session_start additional_context, prepended above all other sections
decisions.rationale: session_start is the only cursor hook that can inject context, and it fires once per session. Putting the warning at the TOP makes it the first thing the agent reads — and a stale binary invalidates every later section in the snapshot, so reading order matters. The warning is a HINT not an error; the hook still runs whatever logic the binary has.

decisions.point: How wide to make HookFacade for the staleness method
decisions.chose: Add BinaryStaleness() as a third method on the existing narrow interface
decisions.rationale: BinaryStaleness is pure mechanical (file stat + git commit date), no semantic judgement, so it stays inside the documented 'narrow surface' rule. The whole point of HookFacade is to make adding any semantic shortcut a deliberate widening conversation; staleness check is not a semantic shortcut.

decisions.chose: 5-minute tolerance on (main HEAD commit time - binary mtime)
decisions.rationale: 5 minutes is way bigger than any realistic clock skew between commit machine and build machine, and small enough that real stale-by-an-hour binaries still trip the check. Soft warning + 5min tolerance trades a tiny false-negative window (binary built within 5min of main HEAD) for a near-zero false-positive rate.

decisions.point: How to handle int_89dab5f5 (auto-Start regression's victim)
decisions.chose: Abandon with reason explaining it was auto-started by stale binary
decisions.rationale: The whole point of the stale-binary fix is to prevent more of these. Abandoning with a clear reason creates a paper trail and confirms the diagnosis.

rejected.alternative: Per-commit hook-wrapper binary embedding via ldflags
rejected.reason: would require build-script changes; mtime check is good enough
too aggressive; user may have pinned an old binary deliberately

rejected.alternative: Detect rebase via git reflog search
rejected.reason: reflog is per-repo and per-clone, not portable across machines

risks: Subject strategy could pin wrong commit if two main commits share a subject (rare in conventional-commit projects; mitigated by iterating in lookback order so most-recent wins, which matches the GitHub PR merge UI semantics)
Stale-binary check fires false positive when main HEAD is doc-only past binary build time (cost: one extra warning line; user can ignore)

BinaryStaleness widens HookFacade by one method; if more such 'mechanical' methods get added without guard, the narrow-surface invariant erodes (mitigation: each addition is reviewed against the rule documented in hooks_facade.go)
followups: Consider scoping stale-binary check to commits that touch *.go|go.mod, dropping doc-only false positives
Surface auto-pinned strategy distribution in Status so users can see how often each strategy fires (helpful for tuning the cascade)
Investigate whether `mainline show` should prefer view data over draft data when sealed/proposed (separate from this PR; flagged during the diagnostic but out of scope)

architectural_claims: HookFacade stays narrow but admits new pure-mechanical methods
Pin cascade is ordered by precision (tree_hash > commit_hash > subject > goal_text)
session_start additional_context is the canonical surface for hook -> agent state hints
Hooks must not import engine; staleness detection uses a structural interface
behavioral_changes: Pin can now match an intent's code_commit to a rebased main commit by exact subject equality
Sync's auto-pin pass can mark intents as merged via match_strategy=subject
Cursor session_start additional_context begins with a stale-binary warning when binary mtime predates main HEAD by >5min
BinaryStaleness() failures are absorbed silently — staleness check never blocks a session

api_changes.signature: engine.pinStrategies appended `subject` between commit_hash and goal_text

migration_notes: No schema bump; the stale-binary warning is purely additive context. Existing hook installations work without re-installing — they just don't get the warning until the binary is rebuilt with this commit.
